title: Solo Travel in China: Safety Tips
metaDescription: Stay safe on your solo trip to China with these essential, practical tips for a smooth and enjoyable journey.

sections.heading: h1
sections.content: Welcome to your solo adventure in China! This vast country is incredibly safe for travelers, but a little preparation ensures a smooth journey.

sections.heading: h2
sections.content: Daily Safety is straightforward. Use official ride-hailing apps like DiDi for transport, keep valuables secure, and learn a few basic Mandarin phrases. China's efficient public transport is generally very safe.

sections.heading: h3
sections.content: Stay Connected & Prepared. Get a local SIM card or eSIM for maps and translation apps. Save your hotel's address in Chinese. Always carry a photocopy of your passport's main page.

keyTakeaways: Use licensed taxis or DiDi; avoid unmarked vehicles.
Keep your passport, cash, and cards in separate, secure locations.
Learn to say 'hello' (Nǐ hǎo) and 'thank you' (Xiè xie).
